mission.title: WxCC Developer Intelligence Hub
mission.summary: Tracking the latest Webex CC API samples, SDK updates, flow templates, webhook patterns, and developer tooling across GitHub.
focusAreas: Desktop SDK widgets and CRM integrations
Flow Designer templates and HTTP node patterns
AI Agent and MCP developer tooling

featureLanes.name: REST API
featureLanes.whyItMatters: The Webex CC REST API is the primary integration surface for tasks, agents, queues, and configuration management.
featureLanes.watchFor: New API endpoints, updated authentication patterns, task management samples, and configuration automation scripts.
featureLanes.businessUse: Automate contact center provisioning, build reporting dashboards, and integrate with CRM systems.
featureLanes.buildPath: Start with OAuth 2.0 Service App setup, then explore /tasks, /agents, /queues endpoints with sample code from WebexSamples.

featureLanes.name: Webhook
featureLanes.whyItMatters: Webhooks deliver real-time events for task lifecycle, agent state, and queue activity to your application.
featureLanes.watchFor: New event types, HMAC verification patterns, retry handling samples, and webhook listener examples.
featureLanes.businessUse: Power real-time dashboards, trigger CRM updates on task creation, and build automated workflow responses.
featureLanes.buildPath: Set up a webhook endpoint with HMAC verification, subscribe to task:created and agent:state events.

featureLanes.name: Desktop SDK
featureLanes.whyItMatters: The Desktop JS SDK lets you embed custom panels and CRM screen pops directly inside the agent desktop iFrame.
featureLanes.watchFor: New SDK events, custom widget examples, screen pop patterns, and CRM integration samples.
featureLanes.businessUse: Give agents instant CRM context, build AI assistant panels, and automate after-call work.
featureLanes.buildPath: Load the Desktop SDK, subscribe to task:incoming events, and render custom UI inside the iFrame widget.

featureLanes.name: Flow Designer
featureLanes.whyItMatters: Flow Designer's HTTP Request node lets flows call external APIs mid-IVR for intelligent, data-driven routing.
featureLanes.watchFor: New flow templates, HTTP node patterns, AI integration flows, variable handling examples, and routing logic.
featureLanes.businessUse: Route calls based on CRM data, authenticate callers via API, and deliver personalised IVR experiences.
featureLanes.buildPath: Use the HTTP Request node to call your API, map response variables, and use them in Queue Contact or Branch nodes.

featureLanes.name: Reporting
featureLanes.whyItMatters: The Reporting API delivers real-time queue stats, historical metrics, and CDRs for BI and analytics pipelines.
featureLanes.watchFor: New reporting endpoints, CDR field updates, real-time subscription patterns, and export automation samples.
featureLanes.businessUse: Build live supervisor dashboards, export data to BigQuery or Power BI, and automate SLA reporting.
featureLanes.buildPath: Start with the /analyzerService/api endpoints for historical data and real-time subscriptions for live stats.

playbooks.title: New API Sample Review
playbooks.objective: Evaluate a newly discovered API sample for inclusion in the Dev Corner samples library.
steps: Open the repository and read the README to understand the use case and scope.
Check the authentication method used — personal token vs OAuth 2.0 Service App.
Run or trace the code to understand the API endpoints and data flow.
Identify the business value — does it solve a common developer pain point?
Note any gaps, issues, or improvements before recommending it.
playbooks.outcome: A clear recommendation on whether to feature the sample and what context to add.

playbooks.title: Flow Template Assessment
playbooks.objective: Assess a new Flow Designer template for reusability and documentation quality.
steps: Download or inspect the flow JSON from the repository.
Identify the flow entry point, key nodes, and exit conditions.
Check whether HTTP Request nodes are used and what external APIs they call.
Test the flow in a sandbox Webex CC environment if possible.
Document the business use case, prerequisites, and customisation points.
playbooks.outcome: A documented flow template ready to feature in the Dev Corner with usage guidance.

playbooks.title: Weekly Developer Update
playbooks.objective: Summarise the week's most relevant developer discoveries for sharing with the team.
steps: Review the What Changed tab for all new and updated repositories this week.
Identify the top 3 items most relevant to REST API, Webhook, or SDK developers.
Check Discovery tab for any community repos worth highlighting.
Write a short summary with links and a one-line reason each item matters.
Share via your team channel or update the Dev Corner samples page.
playbooks.outcome: A concise weekly developer digest that keeps the team current without noise.

glossary.meaning: Webex CC HTTP API for managing tasks, agents, queues, sites, teams, and configuration resources.
Real-time HTTP push notifications for contact center events like task:created and agent:state.
JavaScript SDK for embedding custom widgets inside the Webex CC agent desktop iFrame.
Visual IVR builder with HTTP Request nodes for calling external APIs mid-flow.
Machine-to-machine OAuth 2.0 application type for production API integrations without user login.
Hash-based message authentication used to verify webhook payloads come from Webex CC.
Model Context Protocol — standard for giving AI agents access to tools and data sources.
Agent-to-Agent protocol for multi-agent orchestration and handoff between AI systems.
Call Detail Record — post-call data containing duration, queue, agent, and outcome fields.
Custom web application embedded in the agent desktop via the Desktop SDK iFrame mechanism.

sourceTypes.name: WebexSamples
sourceTypes.purpose: Official Cisco/Webex GitHub org for Webex Contact Center code samples, SDKs, and flow templates.

sourceTypes.name: CiscoDevNet
sourceTypes.purpose: Cisco developer network GitHub org for broader Webex and Cisco platform samples.

sourceTypes.name: Community Repos
sourceTypes.purpose: GitHub repositories from partners, customers, and developers building on the Webex CC platform.

sourceTypes.name: MCP Ecosystem
sourceTypes.purpose: Model Context Protocol repositories relevant to building AI-capable contact center integrations.

sourceTypes.name: A2A Project
sourceTypes.purpose: Agent-to-agent protocol repositories relevant to future multi-agent Webex CC architectures.
